title: QUIC RFC 9001 Repeated 1-RTT Key Update Lifecycle Work Item
summary: Plan the trace-safe widening from the current first-successor Key Phase floor to a repeated 1-RTT key-update lifecycle, including old-key retention and discard, without treating the existing first-update helper as full RFC 9001 support.
planned_changes:
- Preserve the bounded repeated-update control state that now exists above the first-successor floor for `REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P5-0003`: current-phase acknowledgment tracking and the three-PTO post-confirmation local-update cooldown.
- Preserve the bounded repeated local install floor that now derives and installs phase-2 material only after current-phase acknowledgment, three-PTO cooldown, and retained-old first-update discard, and emits outbound Key Phase using key-phase parity.
- Preserve the bounded repeated lower-recovered receive-selection floor that now authenticates retained phase-1 packets after local phase-2 install, rejects retained phase-1 packets carrying the phase-0 bit, and tracks the current packet-number floor per key phase.
- Preserve the timing-neutral invalid-Key-Phase classification now proven for `REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P3-0001` and the current timing-neutral no-close policy for apparent unconfirmed consecutive peer updates under `REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P2-0003`.
- Preserve the helper-backed AEAD key lifecycle and policy floor now proven for `REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P6-0001` through `REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P6-0005`: per-key-set counters, confidentiality-limit stop-use, update-required classification, connection stop-use classification, and stateless-reset-only classification.
- Preserve the bounded runtime AEAD-limit wiring now added to 1-RTT send/open paths: first-update-before-excess-protection, successor-protection discard, and receive-side integrity-limit discard without close-datagram emission.
- Preserve the existing first-successor install path as a bounded prerequisite rather than widening receive-side repeated epoch selection in place.
- Keep repeated AEAD-limit-triggered updates, repeated old-key discard cleanup beyond the newly retained phase-1 floor, and broader stateless-reset-only response behavior blocked until those follow-ons have explicit requirements and proof plans.
- Keep TLS KeyUpdate prohibition, post-handshake ticket ingestion, 0-RTT, public API promises, and interop expansion out of this RFC 9001 lifecycle slice unless separately traced.
verification_plan: The executable slice must include positive tests for repeated local and peer updates, negative tests for duplicate/stale/tampered transitions, current-phase acknowledgment gating, packet-number-incoherent old-key packets, AEAD-limit-driven update requests, and premature discard, fuzz or property coverage for Key Phase observation and epoch transition boundaries, and BenchmarkDotNet coverage for hot successor-derivation/install/open and AEAD lifecycle policy paths. The current first-update tests, S6P6 runtime wiring tests, QuicApplicationPacketKeyPhaseBenchmarks, QuicRepeatedKeyUpdateControlBenchmarks, and QuicAeadKeyLifecycleBenchmarks remain bounded evidence only.
completion_notes: Planned for the broader repeated lifecycle. The direct first-successor proof for REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P1-0001 through REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P1-0010 is now executable at the requirement-home level, including retained old first-update 1-RTT packet-protection material before and after new-key packet authentication plus explicit retained cleanup. The first peer-initiated update proof now covers REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P2-0001, REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P2-0002, REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P2-0003, and REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P2-0004 for opening next-key packets only when they carry the next Key Phase bit, updating send keys before ACK emission, dropping apparent unconfirmed consecutive peer updates without close/datagram signaling under the current optional policy, and allowing KEY_UPDATE_ERROR when a retained old-key ACK packet acknowledges a tracked first-successor phase-1 send. The invalid Key Phase timing-neutral proof now covers REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P3-0001 by dropping current/next key-phase mismatches without close/datagram signaling or successor installation. The first next-receive-key retention proof now covers REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P3-0002 by retaining current and next receive material for apparent peer updates and clearing the retained next material once the first successor is installed. The first send-key ordering proof now covers REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P4-0001 and REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P4-0002 for first-update ACK sends and locally built higher-packet-number packets. The first old/current next receive-selection proof now covers REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P4-0003, REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P5-0001, and REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P5-0002 for rejecting old-key packets that violate the first successor packet-number floor, using retained old receive material when recovered packet numbers fall below the current phase floor, and using next receive material when recovered packet numbers rise above that floor. The repeated local-update proof now covers REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P5-0003 by tracking acknowledgment of the current key phase, exposing a three-PTO post-confirmation eligibility gate, requiring retained-old first-update discard before phase-2 install, and emitting outbound Key Phase by phase parity. The repeated lower-recovered receive-selection proof now extends REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P5-0001 by authenticating lower recovered phase-1 packets after local phase-2 install, rejecting retained phase-1 packets with the phase-0 bit, and isolating the current packet-number floor by key phase. The repeated peer second-successor proof now extends REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P2-0001, REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P2-0002, REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P2-0003, REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P3-0002, and REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P5-0002 by accepting peer phase-2 packets only after current-phase confirmation and prior old-key discard, retaining second-successor receive material for apparent repeated peer updates, sending ACKs with phase-2 keys, and preserving timing-neutral drops for unconfirmed consecutive peer updates. The first retained-old discard proof now covers REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P5-0004 and REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P5-0005 by arming a three-PTO retained-old discard timer after the first authenticated phase-1 packet and discarding the retained old read material together with matching sender and recovery state when that timer expires. The helper-backed AEAD key lifecycle, policy, and bounded runtime proof now covers REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P6-0001 through REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P6-0005 for per-key-set protected/opened-packet counting, confidentiality-limit stop-use, update-required classification, connection stop-use classification, stateless-reset-only classification, first AEAD-limit-triggered key update on send, successor-protection discard, and receive-side integrity-limit discard without close-datagram emission. The remaining planned work is repeated AEAD-limit-triggered updates, broader stateless-reset-only response behavior, and repeated old-key discard cleanup beyond the newly retained phase-1 floor; no general repeated 1-RTT key-update implementation is claimed by this artifact.

supplemental_sections.heading: Current Boundary
supplemental_sections.content: The current executable floor can prove the first 0-to-1 successor install, successor write-key use, stable header-protection keys, updated-key protection for subsequent packets, pre-confirmation local-update rejection, bounded repeat-update rejection before any current-phase acknowledgment support exists, first current-phase acknowledgment tracking, the first three-PTO post-confirmation repeated local-update cooldown, timing-neutral invalid Key Phase drop classification, timing-neutral no-close handling for apparent unconfirmed consecutive peer updates, successor receive-key installation, retained next first-update receive material, retained old first-update 1-RTT material before and after new-key packet authentication, the first retained-old discard timer after authenticating a new-key packet, first old-key discard with matching sender/recovery cleanup, first peer-initiated next-key packet opening, updated send-key ACK protection for peer-initiated updates, the first KEY_UPDATE_ERROR floor for retained old-key ACK packets that acknowledge first-successor phase-1 sends, first-update send-key ordering, the first old/current next receive-selection floors for lower and higher recovered packet numbers, the first KEY_UPDATE_ERROR floor for old-key packet-number ordering violations, helper-backed per-key-set protected/opened-packet counting, confidentiality-limit stop-use, AEAD-limit policy classification, bounded runtime first-update-or-discard send/open wiring, QUIC `quic ku` successor traffic-secret derivation, and packet key/IV derivation from updated secrets. Bounded local and peer phase-2 cycles after acknowledgment are now proven; repeated AEAD-limit-triggered update requests after the first successor, broader stateless-reset-only terminal behavior, and repeated old 1-RTT key discard beyond the newly retained phase-1 floor remain planned until the lifecycle owner is implemented and proven.

supplemental_sections.heading: Traffic Secret Advancement Slice
supplemental_sections.content:
- Add a pending 1-RTT traffic-secret update carrier so derived successor secrets are zeroed unless the matching bridge-state install commits.
- Replace stored application traffic secrets with the updated `quic ku` secrets when the first local or peer-initiated update commits.
- Allow helper derivation of the next successor material from the advanced secrets for bounded local and peer phase-2 install paths while keeping repeated AEAD-limit-triggered updates and broader cleanup blocked.

supplemental_sections.heading: Bounded Repeated Local Install Slice
supplemental_sections.content:
- Install a repeated local update only after current-phase acknowledgment, three-PTO cooldown, and retained-old first-update discard.
- Retain the displaced phase-1 open/protect material when phase 2 becomes current.
- Emit outbound Key Phase using key-phase parity so phase 2 writes bit 0.
- Keep repeated AEAD-limit-triggered updates, repeated old-key discard cleanup beyond the newly retained phase-1 floor, and broader stateless-reset-only response behavior blocked.

supplemental_sections.heading: Bounded Repeated Lower-Recovered Receive Slice
supplemental_sections.content:
- Authenticate lower recovered phase-1 packets with retained previous material after bounded local phase-2 install.
- Reject retained phase-1 packets that carry the phase-0 bit after phase 2 becomes current.
- Track the current packet-number floor by key-phase counter so first-update phase-1 observations do not poison phase-2 ordering.
- Keep repeated AEAD-limit-triggered updates and repeated old-key discard cleanup beyond the newly retained phase-1 floor blocked.

supplemental_sections.heading: Bounded Repeated Peer Second-Successor Slice
supplemental_sections.content:
- Retain second-successor receive material after current-phase confirmation and prior retained-old discard.
- Authenticate peer phase-2 packets with Key Phase bit 0 and install matching phase-2 open/protect material.
- Send ACKs for repeated peer-updated packets with phase-2 send keys.
- Preserve timing-neutral drops for unconfirmed consecutive peer updates.
- Keep repeated AEAD-limit-triggered updates, repeated old-key discard cleanup beyond the newly retained phase-1 floor, and broader stateless-reset-only response behavior blocked.

supplemental_sections.heading: Next Planned Scope
supplemental_sections.content:
- Design and prove repeated AEAD-limit-triggered local updates or repeated old-key discard cleanup now that bounded local and peer phase-2 installs are implemented.
- Keep broader stateless-reset-only response behavior blocked until its requirements are traced.
